title: Memory Addresses
description: Understand where data lives in the computer's memory.

slides.title: Inside the RAM 💾
slides.content: When a variable is created in C++, a memory address is automatically assigned to it in the computer's RAM. The variable name is simply a friendly human name that points to this physical location.

slides.title: Accessing the Address
slides.content: You can find out *exactly* where your variable physically lives in memory by putting an ampersand `&` directly in front of the variable name.
slides.codeSnippet:
string food = "Pizza";

cout << &food; // Outputs something like 0x6dfed4

quizData.question: What is the weird output `0x6dfed4`?

options.text: It is the physical memory address (in hexadecimal format) where 'Pizza' is stored in RAM
It is a random number generator

quizData.explanation: Because we used `&food`, we printed its true physical RAM location, known as the "memory address".

slides.title: Why we care 🏎️
slides.content:
Why is this important?

Because understanding memory allows us to manipulate our data much faster and with far less memory overhead, which is why C++ is used for high-performance apps like heavy 3D video games!

quizData.question: Using the `&` operator in front of a variable allows you to see its physical RAM address.
